sourceMovie.title: Spring
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Spring (2014) as a horror theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The horror and romance blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Thing
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Spring clicked for you, The Thing (1982) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and science fiction, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Spring?
faq.answer: Try The Thing, The Fly, Eraserhead—each shares DNA with Spring while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Spring?
faq.answer: Spring (2014) is best described as Horror, Romance, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Spring worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.6/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this horror lane.
